workflows.name: New Residential Inquiry to Installation
workflows.trigger: Homeowner contacts Nimbus about a potential installation

steps.action: Initial inquiry — homeowner contacts Nimbus via phone (940-252-4785), email (info@nimbusgroundeffects.com), or website contact form
steps.notes: Nimbus does not accept walk-ins. All inquiries start by phone, email, or form.

steps.action: Phone screening — Nimbus asks basic qualifying questions: property location (confirm service area), approximate yard size, surface type, proximity to pool or HVAC, and HOA status. This call takes 10 to 15 minutes.
steps.notes: If the property is clearly outside service area or has an obvious hard disqualifier, Nimbus will advise the homeowner during this call rather than proceeding to a paid site assessment.

steps.action: Site assessment scheduled — if phone screening does not reveal disqualifiers, Nimbus schedules an on-site feasibility survey ($150 residential, credited toward installation)
steps.expected_timeframe: Site assessment typically scheduled within 5 to 10 business days of inquiry

steps.action: Site assessment conducted — Marcus or Danny visits the property for 60 to 90 minutes. Grade measurement, drainage evaluation, setback verification, electrical and water access check, and zone layout planning.
steps.notes: Homeowner or authorized representative must be present to discuss preferences, identify any underground utilities or irrigation lines, and provide access to all areas of the property.

steps.action: Feasibility report delivered — written report with findings, recommended system type, zone layout diagram, any prerequisite work needed, and a detailed quote
steps.expected_timeframe: Within 3 business days of site assessment

steps.action: Contract signing and deposit — homeowner reviews quote, signs contract, and submits 50 percent deposit
steps.notes: Quote valid for 30 days. No work is scheduled until deposit is received.

steps.action: Pre-installation coordination — Nimbus confirms electrical circuit is installed (by homeowner's electrician), HOA approval is documented (if applicable), and any prerequisite drainage or grading work is complete
Installation — crew arrives, trenches manifold, installs nozzles, places control unit, charges fluid, and runs full system test

Commissioning and homeowner walkthrough — Marcus or Danny demonstrates system operation, programs scheduling, sets up app (Premium), walks through maintenance basics, and obtains homeowner sign-off. Balance payment due at this step.
steps.notes: Homeowner sign-off triggers the start of all warranty periods.

workflows.name: Maintenance Service Request
workflows.trigger: Homeowner reports a system issue or requests a scheduled maintenance visit

steps.action: Homeowner calls Nimbus service line (940-252-4785) or emails service@nimbusgroundeffects.com
Nimbus triages the issue — determines whether it is routine maintenance, a calibration request, or a potential system fault

Service visit scheduled. Maintenance plan clients receive 48-hour response time during operating season (March through November). Non-plan clients are scheduled on a next-available basis, typically within 5 to 10 business days.
On-site service performed and documented

workflows.name: Emergency Fluid Leak Response
workflows.trigger: Homeowner observes active fluid leak from control unit or manifold, or receives a low-fluid alert on the Nimbus app (Premium systems)

steps.action: Homeowner shuts down the system using the control panel power switch or the app emergency stop button
steps.notes: The system has automatic low-pressure shutoff, but manual shutoff prevents additional fluid loss.

steps.action: Homeowner calls Nimbus emergency line (940-252-4785). This line is monitored during operating season (March through November), 7 AM to 8 PM, 7 days a week.
steps.notes: The fog fluid is non-toxic and poses no environmental or health hazard. A leak is a maintenance issue, not a safety emergency.

steps.action: Nimbus dispatches a technician. Maintenance plan clients with active leaks receive same-day or next-morning response during operating season. Non-plan clients receive priority scheduling within 48 hours.
Technician identifies leak source, performs repair, refills fluid, and pressure-tests the system before re-commissioning

workflows.name: Commercial Event Booking
workflows.trigger: Event venue or planner contacts Nimbus for a temporary fog installation

steps.action: Venue or planner contacts Nimbus with event date, location, approximate coverage area, and desired effect
Nimbus confirms venue is within service area, verifies power and water access, and provides a preliminary quote within 2 business days
If proceeding, Nimbus conducts a site visit (included in event rental fee for venues not previously assessed) to finalize layout and logistics
Contract signed with full payment for single-event rentals, or 50 percent deposit for seasonal installations
Setup day — Nimbus deploys surface-mounted manifold, positions control unit, charges and tests system. A Nimbus technician remains on-site for the first 2 hours of operation to confirm performance.
steps.notes: Setup typically takes 4 to 8 hours depending on scale. Nimbus requires full-day access on the setup day.

steps.action: Teardown — Nimbus removes all equipment within 24 hours of event conclusion unless otherwise arranged

general_inquiries.hours: Monday through Friday, 8 AM to 5 PM Central

service_requests.hours: Monday through Friday, 8 AM to 5 PM Central

emergency_line.hours: March through November, 7 AM to 8 PM Central, 7 days a week
emergency_line.note: For active fluid leaks or system faults during operating season only. Not monitored December through February.

contact_information.note: Nimbus does not have a showroom or walk-in office. All consultations are by appointment at the client's property.
